sourceMovie.title: PSYCHO-PASS: The Movie
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit PSYCHO-PASS: The Movie for the same reason it broke through in 2015: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a science fiction rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Naoyoshi Shiotani keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ghost in the Shell 2.0
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If PSYCHO-PASS: The Movie clicked for you, Ghost in the Shell 2.0 (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and science fiction, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after PSYCHO-PASS: The Movie?
faq.answer: Try Ghost in the Shell 2.0, Appleseed Alpha, Appleseed—each shares DNA with PSYCHO-PASS: The Movie while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is PSYCHO-PASS: The Movie?
faq.answer: PSYCHO-PASS: The Movie (2015) is best described as Animation, Science Fiction, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is PSYCHO-PASS: The Movie part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Psycho Pass Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
